channel.title: What Is Being Built — Daily Activity Feed
channel.description: Daily updates on open-source automated research tools: commits, releases, and community activity.

item.title: Daily digest — 2026-06-30
item.description:
Our community has seen significant progress across multiple projects recently. Notably, the `giladfeldman/docpluck` project has released version `v2.4.99`, featuring a region-driven table capture and content-plausibility guards, which will likely enhance its integration with other tools like `giladfeldman/escicheck`. Speaking of `escicheck`, its `main` branch has been updated to sync with the latest `effectcheck` versions, ensuring it stays current. Meanwhile, `JamieCummins/regcheck` has undergone hardening for multi-study extractions and improved report text normalization, and `giovannacdelsordo/output-it-forward` is now accessible via the Chrome Webstore. Additionally, `shaheedazaad/auto-zcurve` has revised its validation note and added a citation, demonstrating the community's commitment to rigorous research and documentation. These updates highlight the interconnected nature of our projects and the community's dedication to advancing open-source research tools.

34 commits across 4 active repositories this week.

Most active:

regcheck14 commits
metacheck10 commits
docpluck7 commits
escicheck3 commits

item.title: docpluck: 3 commits today
item.description:
giladfeldman/docpluck

The `main` branch was the focus of the recent work, where a version bump to `v2.4.99` was made, including a "region-driven table capture" as noted in the commit message "chore(release): v2.4.99 — region-driven table capture (version bump + CHANGELOG)". A fix was applied to correct "stale PDFextractor/docpluck paths" to point to the "MetaScienceTools/" layout, as described in the commit "fix(skills): correct stale PDFextractor/docpluck paths to MetaScienceTools/ layout". The "feat(tables): region-driven Camelot capture + content-plausibility guards (DP-1/DP-2)" commit added a region-driven Camelot capture feature along with content-plausibility guards for DP-1 and DP-2. Additionally, a fix was made to tables in the commit "fix(tables): v2.4.98 — DP-3 η²p-from-structure, DP-5 r-cell + bracket-CI (ESCIcheck handoff; flatten-only, AI-gold-verified)", which addressed specific issues related to DP-3 and DP-5.

3 commit(s) today:

  • 03e5d55 [main] chore(release): v2.4.99 — region-driven table capture (version bump + CHANGELOG) — Gilad Feldman
  • b0fdd5f [main] fix(skills): correct stale PDFextractor/docpluck paths to MetaScienceTools/ layout — Gilad Feldman
  • fd072b8 [main] feat(tables): region-driven Camelot capture + content-plausibility guards (DP-1/DP-2) — Gilad Feldman
  • b6bd65e [main] fix(tables): v2.4.98 — DP-3 η²p-from-structure, DP-5 r-cell + bracket-CI (ESCIcheck handoff; flatten-only, AI-gold-verified) — Gilad Feldman
  • 521089a [main] feat(tables): RC-T char-level column-detection fallback for tight-kerned tables (v2.4.98, foundation) — Gilad Feldman
  • 976af30 [main] docs(iterate): 2026-06-25 run handoff + LEARNINGS + project lessons (PARTIAL) — Gilad Feldman
  • 59cff5b [main] fix(tooling): direction-aware check_app_pin_sync note + RC-T/RC-1 spec findings — Gilad Feldman
  • 3e9cc2c [feat/rc-t-table-region-guard] docs(learnings): RC-T Layer-2 + combined-ship closeout (cycle 2, v2.4.97) — Gilad Feldman
  • 813aa4c [feat/rc-t-table-region-guard] fix(tables): v2.4.97 — DP-2 blank p/df + DP-5 two-header-row recovery/super-header alignment + RC-T Layer-2 raw_text prose-trim/suppress — Gilad Feldman
  • 84a4d42 [feat/rc-t-table-region-guard] fix(render): RC-T — strip Camelot tables that are absorbed body prose (v2.4.96) — Gilad Feldman
  • 7247285 [feat/rc-t-table-region-guard] docs(handoff+todo): RC-T implementation handoff + log RC-T deferral / close collabra.77859 adjudication — Gilad Feldman
  • 927d869 [feat/rc-t-table-region-guard] docs(spec): RC-T table-region prose-contamination — confirmed 2-layer root cause + fix plan — Gilad Feldman
  • db7192b [feat/rc-t-table-region-guard] docs(triage+learnings): v2.4.95 corpus assessment — 7/7 canary FAIL, RC-B7 already done (W0h), RC-T root-caused — Gilad Feldman
  • 2dbdd98 [feat/rc-t-table-region-guard] docs+tooling: enforce library↔app version-pin sync (directive + qa/review/deploy gate) — Gilad Feldman
  • c1fa68b [feat/rc-t-table-region-guard] docs(todo): log v2.4.95 / REQUEST_11 shipped + deferred follow-ups (effect_type opt-in, 77859 caption-number, text-layer caveat) — Gilad Feldman
  • 370b89c [feat/rc-t-table-region-guard] docs(handoff): record the cross-session skill-file commit coordination (now fulfilled in b2c6aa2) — Gilad Feldman
  • b2c6aa2 [feat/rc-t-table-region-guard] chore(docs+deploy): prior-session housekeeping — REQUEST_10 customer broadcast, daily-digest cron-retry lesson, deploy smoke-gate fix — Gilad Feldman
  • bce4b7f [feat/rc-t-table-region-guard] feat(tables): REQUEST_11 — flatten fields for non-clinical result tables (v2.4.95) — Gilad Feldman
  • 83ab6bf [feat/rc-t-table-region-guard] docs(deploy): document pre-push canary hook timing — never timeout/kill the push — Gilad Feldman
  • 1158b1b [feat/rc-t-table-region-guard] feat(tables): REQUEST_10 — HTTP-flatten enabling quality fixes + cross-flavor capture (v2.4.94) — Gilad Feldman

View all commits →

item.title: escicheck: 1 commit today
item.description:
giladfeldman/escicheck

The `main` branch was the focus of the recent work, with two commits made to it. The first commit, made on June 29, 2026, synced the repository with `escicheckapp@26f8430`, which corresponds to `effectcheck v0.6.8`, as stated in the commit message. The previous day, June 28, 2026, another commit was made to the `main` branch, syncing it with `escicheckapp@57a791d`, which is equivalent to `effectcheck v0.6.7`. These commits brought the repository up to date with the specified versions of `effectcheck`. The commits were made by Gilad Feldman, with issue numbers `#22` and `#21` referenced, respectively.

1 commit(s) today:

  • 75ba643 [main] sync from escicheckapp@26f8430 (effectcheck v0.6.8) (#22) — Gilad Feldman
  • a154bdb [main] sync from escicheckapp@57a791d (effectcheck v0.6.7) (#21) — Gilad Feldman
  • 244b2d8 [main] sync from escicheckapp@f37b5a1 (effectcheck v0.6.6) (#20) — Gilad Feldman
  • a588210 [main] sync from escicheckapp@bd6f007 (effectcheck v0.6.5) (#19) — Gilad Feldman
  • 1d48c4e [main] sync from escicheckapp@dec37b1 (effectcheck v0.6.4) (#18) — Gilad Feldman
  • 4be3331 [main] sync from escicheckapp@f52d7ce (effectcheck v0.6.3) (#17) — Gilad Feldman

View all commits →
